definition: A set of circumstances that makes it possible to do something
example: Yes we can, to opportunity and prosperity. Yes we can heal this nation.
speech: New Hampshire Primary Concession / 'Yes We Can' speech

definition: The state of being prosperous
example: Yes we can, to opportunity and prosperity. Yes we can heal this nation.
speech: New Hampshire Primary Concession / 'Yes We Can' speech

definition: A willingness to take bold risks
example: The audacity of hope! In the end, that is God's greatest gift to us, the bedrock of this nation.

definition: A feeling of expectation and desire for a particular thing to happen
example: The audacity of hope! In the end, that is God's greatest gift to us, the bedrock of this nation.

definition: A thing composed of many different elements
example: Our patchwork heritage is a strength, not a weakness.

definition: Property that is or may be inherited; an inheritance
example: Our patchwork heritage is a strength, not a weakness.

definition: A person who believes that people are motivated purely by self-interest
example: The cynics may say that we will never build a more perfect union. But the cynics are wrong.

definition: The action or fact of joining or being joined
example: The cynics may say that we will never build a more perfect union. But the cynics are wrong.

definition: The events that will necessarily happen to a particular person or thing in the future
example: We are the ones we've been waiting for. We are the change that we seek.

definition: A very brief period of time
example: This is our moment. This is our time.

definition: An alternative route that is shorter than the one usually taken
example: Our journey has never been one of short-cuts or settling for less.
speech: Election Night Victory Speech (Grant Park, Chicago)

definition: Lacking courage or determination
example: Our journey has never been one of short-cuts or settling for less. It has not been the path for the faint-hearted.
speech: Election Night Victory Speech (Grant Park, Chicago)

definition: Continuing or long-lasting
example: Let us therefore brace ourselves to our duties, and so bear ourselves that, if the British Empire and its Commonwealth last for a thousand years, men will still say, 'This was their finest hour.'
source: Their Finest Hour (Churchill, but used in context)

definition: The fundamental principles on which something is based
example: The audacity of hope! In the end, that is God's greatest gift to us, the bedrock of this nation.

definition: A thing given willingly to someone without payment
example: The audacity of hope! In the end, that is God's greatest gift to us, the bedrock of this nation.

definition: Persistence in doing something despite difficulty or delay in achieving success
example: Hope is that thing inside us that insists, despite all the evidence to the contrary, that something better awaits us if we have the courage to reach for it.

definition: The available body of facts or information indicating whether a belief or proposition is true or valid
example: Hope is that thing inside us that insists, despite all the evidence to the contrary, that something better awaits us if we have the courage to reach for it.

definition: The ability to do something that frightens one; bravery
example: Hope is that thing inside us that insists, despite all the evidence to the contrary, that something better awaits us if we have the courage to reach for it.

definition: The condition, state, or quality of being free or as free as possible from all flaws or defects
example: The cynics may say that we will never build a more perfect union.

definition: The nonphysical part of a person which is the seat of emotions and character
example: What the cynics fail to understand is that the ground has shifted beneath them — that the stale political arguments that have consumed us for so long no longer apply.
